name: Guadarrama - El Escorial Green Route
name_original: Monte de Abantos – desde El Escorial por el Real Monasterio de San Lorenzo de El Escorial
description: Hike the difficult 9.3-mile Guadarrama - El Escorial Green Route, gaining 2665 feet with panoramic views from Mount Abantos.
region_intro: Hiking around El Escorial offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Guadarrama mountains, northwest of Madrid. The region features a mix of pine forests, open pastures, and rugged mountain trails, providing varied scenery for outdoor activities. Prominent natural features include Mount Abantos and the protected Bosque de la Herrería, which contribute to the area's significant natural beauty. This area provides a range of trails suitable for different fitness levels, from gentle walks to challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around El Escorial The most popular hiking route is Silla de Felipe II and Bosque de la Herrería Loop, an 8.4 miles (13.4 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 52 minutes to complete. This route combines historical viewpoints with lush forest paths. Another top favourite among local hikers is Guadarrama - El Escorial Green Route, a difficult 9.3 miles (14.9 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation gain through mountain terrain, offering expansive views. Local hikers also love the Monte Abantos — circular from the San Lorenzo de El Escorial train station, an 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail leading through pine forests and up to Mount Abantos, often completed in about 5 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around El Escorial is defined by the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de Guadarrama, dense pine forests, and historical landmarks like the Silla de Felipe II. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from leisurely walks to strenuous mountain climbs. The routes in El Escorial are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 1700 hikers have used komoot to explore El Escorial's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in El Escorial?
A: El Escorial offers a diverse network of over 40 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy strolls through forests to challenging mountain ascents within the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking options in El Escorial?
A: Yes, El Escorial provides several easy and family-friendly hiking options. There are 10 routes classified as easy, perfect for a relaxed outing. Many trails, especially those through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957115621/silla-de-felipe-ii-and-bosque-de-la-herreria-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque de la Herrería&lt;/a&gt;, offer gentle terrain and beautiful scenery suitable for all ages.

Q: What kind of challenging hikes can I find in El Escorial?
A: For those seeking a challenge, El Escorial features 13 difficult routes. These often involve significant elevation gain and rugged mountain terrain, such as the ascent to Mount Abantos. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957007240/guadarrama-el-escorial-green-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadarrama - El Escorial Green Route&lt;/a&gt; offer expansive views and a demanding experience.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in El Escorial are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957115621/silla-de-felipe-ii-and-bosque-de-la-herreria-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silla de Felipe II and Bosque de la Herrería Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which combines historical viewpoints with lush forest paths.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking in El Escorial?
A: Hiking in El Escorial offers a blend of natural beauty and historical significance. You can visit the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/740607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seat of Philip II&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views, explore the pine forests of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2505177" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Abantos&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2877011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covacho Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957115621/silla-de-felipe-ii-and-bosque-de-la-herreria-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque de la Herrería&lt;/a&gt; is another protected natural space with lush forests and historical elements.

Q: Is it possible to reach hiking trails in El Escorial using public transport?
A: Yes, some hiking trails are accessible via public transport. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375616645/monte-abantos-circular-from-the-san-lorenzo-de-el-escorial-train-station" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Abantos — circular from the San Lorenzo de El Escorial train station&lt;/a&gt; route conveniently starts directly from the train station, making it easy to access without a car.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in El Escorial?
A: The hiking routes in El Escorial are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from dense pine forests to open mountain views, and the historical landmarks encountered along the way, such as the Silla de Felipe II.

Q: Are there any routes that follow historical paths or old trails?
A: Yes, some routes in El Escorial incorporate historical paths, including ancient Roman roads and traditional livestock trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318146275/camino-de-las-ventas-and-canada-segoviana-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de las Ventas and Cañada Segoviana Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you along such historical routes, connecting you with the region's past.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Escorial?
A: El Escorial offers enjoyable hiking experiences throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with blooming wildflowers or colorful foliage. Summer mornings are also popular, while winter can offer crisp air and unique views, especially at higher elevations, though some trails might require appropriate gear for colder conditions.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to discover on the trails?
A: Yes, the region around El Escorial features natural highlights including waterfalls. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2877011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covacho Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is a notable point of interest for hikers exploring the natural monuments of the area.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking?
A: The Sierra de Guadarrama, where El Escorial is located, is rich in biodiversity. While hiking, you might spot various bird species, including raptors, and potentially deer or wild boar, especially in quieter forest areas. Observing wildlife from a distance ensures their safety and your enjoyment.
tips:
A visit that you cannot miss! The monastery has been a world heritage site since 1984 and is one of the best ways to appreciate and understand the "golden" era of Spain reigned by Philip II.

You can consult the official website for visiting hours and purchase tickets

https://www.patrimonionacional.es/visita/real-monasterio-de-san-lorenzo-de-el-escorial
One of the classic peaks of the Guadarrama mountain range. Easy ascent along the GR-10 and good views from the summit.

It is an essential point of passage in the hiking and mountaineering routes in the Abantos peak because it has fresh water all year round. The detour to the spring is not indicated, it is a path that leaves from the right slope of the mountain track near the crossroads where the path descends from the Abantos summit. In the meadow of the fountain there are marks of the long-distance trail GR-10, which passes through the water pipe and descends to San Lorenzo de El Escorial through the pine forests of the mountain range.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Guadarrama - El Escorial Green Route?
faqs.answer: This specific route is approximately 14.9 kilometers long and typically takes around 5 hours and 48 minutes to complete. Keep in mind that your actual hiking time may vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this route?
faqs.answer: The Guadarrama - El Escorial Green Route is rated as difficult. It involves a significant elevation gain of over 800 meters, including an ascent to Mount Abantos. Hikers should have very good fitness, be sure-footed, and wear sturdy shoes.

faqs.question: Where does the Guadarrama - El Escorial Green Route start and end?
faqs.answer: This particular Komoot route starts near El Escorial, specifically focusing on the ascent to Mount Abantos and passing by the Royal Monastery of San Lorenzo de El Escorial. While the broader 'Green Route' concept connects Guadarrama and El Escorial, this specific tour begins and ends in the El Escorial area.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable landmarks and natural features. Highlights include the magnificent Royal Monastery of San Lorenzo de El Escorial, the summit of Mount Abantos offering panoramic views, and the tranquil Cervunal Spring. The route also provides glimpses of the Valley of Cuelgamuros and its monumental cross.

faqs.question: Is the Guadarrama - El Escorial Green Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is generally dog-friendly. However, as parts of the trail pass through natural areas and potentially near wildlife habitats, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash to ensure their safety and respect local regulations and other hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need specific permits or to pay entrance fees to hike this route, which traverses parts of the Paraje Pintoresco del Pinar de Abantos y Zona de La Herrería. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning nature conservation and fire prevention.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for hiking this route. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring snow and ice to higher sections like Mount Abantos, requiring appropriate gear and caution.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Guadarrama - El Escorial Green Route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, ranging from comfortable forest tracks and meadows to rocky areas, especially as you ascend towards Mount Abantos. You'll pass through pine forests and encounter granite rock formations typical of the Sierra de Guadarrama.

faqs.question: How can I get to the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: The starting point near El Escorial is well-connected to Madrid. You can reach San Lorenzo de El Escorial by interurban bus lines or RENFE Cercanías train services, making it accessible for a day trip. From the town, you can then walk to the specific trailhead.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the 'Ruta Verde Guadarrama - El Escorial' (86%) and also intersects with parts of 'De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa desde el Puerto de la Fuenfría a San Lorenzo del Escorial)' (59%), '[CIMA MA08] Abantos * San Lorenzo del Escorial' (51%), and 'Senda de los Tesoros de Abantos' (30%).

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the route?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in the vicinity of El Escorial, you'll find numerous cafes, pubs, and restaurants in the town itself. It's a great place to refuel and relax before or after your hike.

waypoints.name: Royal Monastery of San Lorenzo de El Escorial
